title: Seizure Prevention in Traumatic Brain Injury With Levetiracetam and Lacosamide

skipped_criteria: Diagnosis of traumatic brain injury: severe, moderate, or mild with high-risk imaging features — severity grading and imaging features are not available in the schema.
Seizure prophylaxis with one of the two study drugs within 24 hours of initial injury — temporal/treatment timing logic cannot be expressed with available fields.
Patients admitted into the trauma team service — admission service type is not a schema field.
Enrolled in another interventional drug study — trial enrollment status is not a schema field.
Any active, witnessed, or unable to rule out seizure prior to prophylaxis initiation — seizure history/status is not a schema field.
Received antiseizure medication (ASM) prior to randomization — prior medication use is not a schema field.
History of seizures — prior medical history field not available in schema.
On anti-epileptic medications for seizures or other indications prior to admission — prior medication use is not a schema field.
Documented history of alcohol withdrawal with CIWA-Ar or mMINDS requiring treatment — alcohol withdrawal history and clinical scale scores are not schema fields.

Spinal cord injury confirmed by radiographic imaging at cervical (C1-C8) or thoracic (T1-T12) levels — imaging confirmation and spinal level specificity cannot be expressed; spinal_injury_suspected used as a soft proxy only.

History of bradycardia or permanent pacemaker — medical history fields are not available in schema; HR < 55 bpm encoded as a proxy but the 'for > 5 min not on medications that cause bradycardia' qualifier cannot be expressed.
End-stage renal disease (ESRD) — prior medical history/lab values not available in schema.
Death, withdrawal of life support, or transfer to hospice within 24 hours — disposition/outcome fields are not available in schema.
Patient incarcerated — incarceration status is not a schema field.

Patient with GCS of 15 or LAR unable to provide informed consent, or unable to read and understand specified languages — consent capacity and language comprehension are not schema fields; GCS 15 exclusion conflicts with inclusion logic and cannot be cleanly encoded.
